mission.title: Install and Configure Valkey
mission.description: Valkey is a high-performance distributed key-value database optimized for caching and real-time workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring fast data access and supports a variety of data structures. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration and potential complexity in deployment.

steps.title: Add the Valkey Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Valkey Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add valkey https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey/releases/download/v9.0.3

```

> This URL points to the specific release version.

steps.title: Install Valkey CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Valkey to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/valkey-io/valkey/v9.0.3/deploy/crds/valkey_crd.yaml

```

> Ensure the CRDs are installed before deploying the application.

steps.title: Install Valkey using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Valkey in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

```

> Replace 'valkey' with your desired release name.

steps.title: Create a Valkey Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource to define your Valkey instance configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Adjust the replicas as needed for your deployment.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Valkey is running
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Valkey deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> You should see the Valkey pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Valkey
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the Valkey deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall valkey --namespace valkey

kubectl delete crd valkeys.valkey.io

```

> This command removes the Helm release.
> This command deletes the Custom Resource Definitions.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Valkey
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining the pods, upgrading, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade valkey valkey/valkey --namespace valkey --version 9.0.3

kubectl get pods -n valkey

```

> Replace <pod-name> with the name of your Valkey pod.
> This command upgrades Valkey to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Valkey server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Valkey server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n valkey' and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Custom Resource Definition not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD for Valkey may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD with 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/valkey-io/valkey/v9.0.3/deploy/crds/valkey_crd.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the Valkey configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n valkey' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the Valkey pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits or add more nodes to the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new caching algorithms and improved data replication features.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy data structures.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from version 8.x, review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all Valkey pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: valkey.io/v1
kind: Valkey
metadata:
  name: my-valkey
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and the necessary tools installed.
